Dojin-kai.description: The Dojin-kai is a yakuza organization based in Kurume, Fukuoka, Japan, founded in 1971 by Isoji Koga. It is a designated boryokudan group with approximately 310 members, known for its militant nature and involvement in large-scale methamphetamine trafficking, which is traditionally shunned in the yakuza world. The group has been involved in violent conflicts, including the Yama-Michi War against the Yamaguchi-gumi, and is a member of the anti-Yamaguchi Yonsha-kai federation.

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Kudo-kai are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kudo-kai, Sinaloa cartel, Taishu-kai, Kumamoto-kai)

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Kumamoto-kai are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kudo-kai, Sinaloa cartel, Taishu-kai, Kumamoto-kai)

descriptions: Dojin-kai and its splinter group Kyushu Seido-kai have been in a violent conflict since 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai again started a blatantly vicious conflict with its splinter group, Kyushu Seido-kai, in 2006.

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Namikawa-kai are rivals per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: The Namikawa-kai and the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Sinaloa cartel are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kudo-kai, Sinaloa cartel, Taishu-kai, Kumamoto-kai)

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Sumiyoshi Union had a brief conflict in 1983.
edges.evidence_quote: Another notable one is a brief conflict that occurred in 1983, with the Kanto-based large yakuza syndicate Sumiyoshi Union, in which a majority of Dojin members moved to Tokyo and had been hiding out around Tokyo with the plan of slaying all of the Union's 76 senior bosses until the Union made a conceding offer of peaceful resolution (teuchi).

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Taishu-kai are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kudo-kai, Sinaloa cartel, Taishu-kai, Kumamoto-kai)

descriptions: Dojin-kai is a designated yakuza group, a subset of Yakuza.
edges.evidence_quote: Fukuoka Prefecture has the largest number of designated yakuza groups among all of the prefectures, at 5; the Kudo-kai , the Taishu-kai , the Fukuhaku-kai , the Dojin-kai , and the Namikawa-kai .

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi are rivals; Seido-kai aligned with Yamaguchi-gumi against Dojin-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai again started a blatantly vicious conflict with its splinter group, Kyushu Seido-kai, in 2006. ... They allegedly aligned themselves with the Yamaguchi-gumi, the Dojin-kai's rival and the largest yakuza syndicate in Japan.

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi are rivals per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: The Namikawa-kai and the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Dojin-kai is a member of the Yonsha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi fraternal federation, the Yonsha-kai, formed with three other northern-Kyushu based independent yakuza syndicates, the Kudo-kai, Taishu-kai and Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Fukuhaku-kai has been in conflict with Dojin-kai over attempts to enter Nakasu territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The Fukuhaku-kai has been in conflict with four different Yamaguchi-affiliates over the concessions of Nakasu , the largest red-light district in Kyushu, and also with the Dojin-kai and Kudo-kai over their attempts to enter the same territory.

descriptions: Kudo-kai and Dojin-kai are both members of the Yonsha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kudo-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi fraternal federation, the Yonsha-kai, with three other northern-Kyushu based organizations, the Taishu-kai , Dojin-kai , and Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai engaged in armed conflicts with its former parent syndicate Dojin-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Since its formation, the Kyushu Seido-kai has been known for its blatant armed conflicts with its former parent syndicate, the Dojin-kai

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai ended its gang war with Dojin-kai and dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: On June 11, 2013, Kyushu Seido-kai announced the end of its gang war with Dojin-kai as well as its dissolution.

descriptions: Taishu-kai and Dojin-kai are both members of the Yonsha-kai federation, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Taishu-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi-gumi fraternal federation, the "Yonsha-kai", along with the Kitakyushu -based Kudo-kai , the Kurume -based Dojin-kai and the Kumamoto -based Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Dojin-kai attacked Toa-kai after Toa-kai refused to cooperate in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: the Dojin-kai attempted to compel the Toa-kai to co-operate in drug trafficking but the Toa-kai rejected it and then the mass attacks started

descriptions: Dojin-kai attempted to expand into Okinawa with heavy violence, contrasting with Toa-kai's gentle stance.
edges.evidence_quote: The reason why the Toa-kai has been able to be active on the island may be because the group has historically been more oriented to legitimate businesses, and of its gentle stance, in contrast to other yakuza syndicates which have attempted to expand their influences into Okinawa with heavy violence, such as the Yamaguchi-gumi and especially the Dojin-kai .

descriptions: Banana gang is a component of the Moroccan mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Belgian authorities dismantled the Belgian-Moroccan Banana gang

descriptions: Guardianes Morelenses is a splinter group of Beltrán Leyva.
edges.evidence_quote: Fragmentación ... - Guardianes Morelenses en Morelos .

'Ndrina Rosmini.description: Powerful 'ndrina (mafia clan) based in Reggio Calabria, controlling the neighborhoods of Ciccarello, Modena, and San Giorgio. Allied with the Condello-Imerti-Serraino group during the second 'ndrangheta war and closely linked to the De Stefano family. Has significant interests in Lombardy and controls public contracts in central and southern Reggio Calabria. Currently led by Diego Rosmini junior.

descriptions: Barreca fought against the Rosmini clan in the second 'Ndrangheta war.
edges.evidence_quote: contro gli Imerti, i Condello, i Saraceno, i Fontana, i Rosmini, i Lo Giudice e i Serraino

descriptions: Condello and Rosmini seceded together from De Stefano.
edges.evidence_quote: Lo scontro inizia nel 1985 con la secessione dei Condello- Rosmini - Imerti dal clan De Stefano

descriptions: The Condello clan was allied with the Rosmini clan.
edges.evidence_quote: I condelliani erano alleati con gli Imerti , i Rosmini , i Fontana e i Saraceno.

descriptions: Rosmini is listed as an allied 'ndrina of Imerti.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Rosmini

descriptions: The Rosmini clan allied with the Imerti clan during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: Con gli Imerti si schierano i Condello , i Saraceno, i Fontana, i Rosmini , i Lo Giudice e i Serraino

descriptions: Lo Giudice allied with Rosmini during the second 'ndrangheta war.
edges.evidence_quote: Nella seconda guerra di 'ndrangheta i Lo Giudice si schierarono con gli Imerti - Condello - Serraino - Rosmini .

descriptions: Rosmini is currently very closely linked to the De Stefano family.
edges.evidence_quote: La famiglia Rosmini attualmente sembra essere molto legata alla famiglia De Stefano.

descriptions: Rosmini and Zindato were both targeted in the same police operation, indicating a link.
edges.evidence_quote: operazione Cemetery Boss contro le cosche Rosmini e Zindato.

descriptions: Rosmini allied with the Condello-Imerti-Serraino group during the second 'ndrangheta war.
edges.evidence_quote: Nella seconda guerra di 'ndrangheta si schierarono con il gruppo Condello - Imerti - Serraino.

descriptions: Serraino sided with Rosmini during the Second 'Ndrangheta war.
edges.evidence_quote: Durante la seconda guerra di 'ndrangheta si schierano con i Condello - Imerti - Rosmini .

descriptions: Rosmini and Zindato were both targeted in the same police operation.
edges.evidence_quote: 26 maggio 2020: Operazione Cemetery Boss contro le cosche Rosmini e Zindato.

descriptions: Rosmini 'ndrina was a rival of De Stefano during the Second 'Ndrangheta war.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Second 'Ndrangheta war (1985-1991): Condello, Imerti, Serraino 'ndrina and Rosmini 'ndrina

descriptions: Latella allied with Rosmini in 2013 to control construction.
edges.evidence_quote: the Latella 'ndrina, together with the Rosmini, Serraino, Audino, and Nicolò 'ndrine, formed an alliance to control the construction sector

descriptions: Both are members of the Northern Alliance, a coalition of insurgent groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance —which consists of four insurgent groups , the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: Both are members of the Northern Alliance, a coalition of insurgent groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance —which consists of four insurgent groups , the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: MNDAA attacked TNLA, breaking their alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In mid-March 2026, the alliance between MNDAA and TNLA broke down over the issue of security cameras and to reopen the trade route to China. The MNDAA attacked TNLA-controlled Kutkai using drones and took the city on 16 March.

descriptions: Both are members of the Northern Alliance, a coalition of insurgent groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance —which consists of four insurgent groups , the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: MNDAA and TNLA conduct joint attacks against the Tatmadaw.
edges.evidence_quote: The MNDAA and Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA) have carried out several joint attacks against the Tatmadaw in Shan State.

descriptions: After conflict, MNDAA and TNLA pledged friendship and cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On the 17th anniversary of the founding of the Arakan Army, the MNDAA and TNLA pledged to continue their friendship, as well as cooperate with the AA and to work together until the 'revolutionary vision' is achieved.

descriptions: Northern Alliance includes TNLA as a member.
edges.evidence_quote: The Northern Alliance is a coalition of four rebel groups – the MNDAA, AA, TNLA, and the KIA – that was founded in 2016

edges.evidence_quote: the TNLA received public support from ... other rebel groups like the KIA

edges.evidence_quote: Prior to the coup, the TNLA clashed regularly with the Restoration Council of Shan State over control of northern Shan state.

descriptions: United Wa State Army provided military support to TNLA.
edges.evidence_quote: the TNLA received public support from ... other rebel groups like the KIA and United Wa State Army

edges.evidence_quote: The Three Brotherhood Alliance ... is an alliance between the Arakan Army, Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army, and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army formed in June 2019.

edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA declared that they would no longer provide weapons or financial aid to the TNLA, MNDAA, and Shan State Army - North

descriptions: Murder, Inc. killed Dutch Schultz and his associates.
edges.evidence_quote: Murder, Inc’s best known victim was most likely Dutch Schultz

descriptions: Outlaws merged with Gypsy Raiders, absorbing them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws merged with the Gypsy Raiders motorcycle gang of Louisville in August 1964.

descriptions: Outlaws merged with the Gypsy Raiders in 1964.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1964, the Outlaws merged with the ... Gypsy Raiders in Louisville, Kentucky

Whyos.description: The Whyos were a dominant street gang in New York City from the late 1860s to the early 1890s, formed from remnants of earlier Five Points gangs. Based in the Bowery, they were predominantly Irish American and engaged in street fighting, murder, robbery, and extortion. The gang was known for requiring members to have committed at least one murder. They were eventually defeated by the Monk Eastman Gang.

descriptions: The Whyos were the main rivals of the Cherry Hill Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Their main rivals were the Whyos

descriptions: Chichesters were absorbed by Whyos after the Civil War.
edges.evidence_quote: before being absorbed by the Whyos

descriptions: Eastman Gang succeeded the Whyos as a prominent gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the Eastman gang succeeded the long dominant Whyos as the first non- Irish street gang to gain prominence in the underworld during the 1890s.

descriptions: Five Points Gang absorbed remaining members of the Whyos.
edges.evidence_quote: Kelly recruited the remaining members of other Five Points gangs, such as the Dead Rabbits and Whyos, into his growing gang.

descriptions: The Whyos included reconstituted members of previous Five Points area gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Another late 19th century New York gang was the Jewish Eastman Gang. Meanwhile, Chinese immigrants formed tongs, which were highly structured gangs involved in gambling and drug trafficking. These tongs were matched in strength by an emerging Italian organized crime network that became the American Mafia.

descriptions: Monk Eastman Gang defeated the Whyos, ending their dominance.
edges.evidence_quote: when the Monk Eastman Gang defeated the last of the Whyos

descriptions: Whyos formed from members of the Chichesters.
edges.evidence_quote: Originally forming from members of the Chichesters

descriptions: Five Points Gang rose to prominence as the Whyos declined, but no direct relationship stated.
edges.evidence_quote: As Monk Eastman and the Five Points Gang came to prominence in the mid-1890s

descriptions: The Fertitta Group succeeded the Maceo Organization after the Maceo brothers' deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: After Sam and Rose's deaths in the early 1950s, the brothers were succeeded by the Fertitta Group

edges.evidence_quote: Carbone crime family (defunct)

descriptions: Crips had disputes with L.A. Brims.

descriptions: Crips made incursions into L.A. Brims territory, leading to formation of Bloods.
edges.evidence_quote: Para responder a las incursiones de los Crips, muchas pandillas pequeñas se unieron y los bloods fueron parte de ellas.

descriptions: SLM (al-Nur) is listed as an opponent of RSF.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... SLM (al-Nur)

descriptions: Many Rock Machine members came from the SS gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The majority of the Rock Machine were former members of the Merciless Riders, the Executeurs and the SS.

descriptions: LRJR collaborated with local jagos (preman) like Haji Darip.
edges.evidence_quote: The formation of the LRJR (Lasykar Rakyat Jakarta Raya, People's Militia of Greater Jakarta) once again saw their collaboration with the local jagos like Haji Darip, Pak Macem and Kyai Haji Nurali

descriptions: Hells Angels aligned with Satans Soldiers.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Hells Angels have aligned themselves with the Coffin Cheaters , Immortals, Red Devils , Satans Soldiers, Vikings and the Prisoners of War

edges.evidence_quote: I contrasti tra gli Schiavone e i De Falco

descriptions: Seven Stars is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Seven Stars, 21 Boys

Terceiro Comando.description: Brazilian criminal organization engaged in drug trafficking in Rio de Janeiro, founded in the early 1980s as a breakaway faction of Comando Vermelho. It operated as a horizontal network without a sole leader, supporting member gangs in favelas. The group was active until 2006 and fought conflicts with Comando Vermelho.

descriptions: ADA initially allied with Terceiro Comando.
edges.evidence_quote: Inicialmente, o Amigos dos Amigos aliou-se ao Terceiro Comando (TC);

descriptions: Comando Vermelho has fought in conflicts with TC.
edges.evidence_quote: It has fought in several small-scale conflicts with rival gangs such as ... Terceiro Comando (TC)

descriptions: Terceiro Comando emerged as a splinter from Comando Vermelho due to internal power struggles.
edges.evidence_quote: the latter itself the result of a power struggle between Comando Vermelho leaders in the mid-1980s.

descriptions: Terceiro Comando and ADA formed an alliance in 1994.
edges.evidence_quote: Com a criação da facção ADA (Amigos dos Amigos) , em 1994 , ambas às organizações firmam uma aliança.

descriptions: TC allied with ADA in 1998.
edges.evidence_quote: Em 1998, o Terceiro Comando (TC) aliou-se a facção recém-criada Amigos dos Amigos (ADA), ampliando seus domínios.

descriptions: Terceiro Comando fought conflicts with Comando Vermelho in 2001 and 2004.
edges.evidence_quote: The Terceiro Comando has fought several small-scale conflicts (in 2001 and 2004) with the rival gang Comando Vermelho.

descriptions: Terceiro Comando was created to oppose Comando Vermelho.
edges.evidence_quote: surgida para se opor ao Comando Vermelho em 1988

descriptions: Terceiro Comando was a break-away faction of Comando Vermelho.
edges.evidence_quote: founded in the early 1980s as a break-away faction of the § Red Command

descriptions: Terceiro Comando was formed as a splinter group from Comando Vermelho.
edges.evidence_quote: It was founded in the early 1980s and is a break-away faction of the Comando Vermelho.

descriptions: Terceiro Comando may have originated as a dissidence from Falange Jacaré.
edges.evidence_quote: o Terceiro Comando assim como o Comando Vermelho pode ter sido criado de uma dissidência de uma das falanges que controlavam a prisão de Ilha Grande, provavelmente a Falange Jacaré

descriptions: Terceiro Comando Puro is a dissident faction of Terceiro Comando.
edges.evidence_quote: Em 2002, surgiu uma dissidência, o Terceiro Comando Puro

descriptions: TCP split from Terceiro Comando in 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: a Rio de Janeiro group that split off from § Terceiro Comando in 2002

descriptions: TCP absorbed members of Terceiro Comando.
edges.evidence_quote: eventually absorbed its parent group's members

descriptions: TCP split off from TC in 2002 and later absorbed its members.
edges.evidence_quote: Terceiro Comando Puro ( Pure Third Command , TCP ) is a Brazilian criminal organization in Rio de Janeiro that split off from the Terceiro Comando (TC) in 2002 due to disputes about TC's affiliations with the Amigos dos Amigos gang.

descriptions: TCP originated as a splinter of Terceiro Comando.
edges.evidence_quote: surgida no Rio de Janeiro, a partir de uma dissidência do Terceiro Comando

Organised crime in the Netherlands.description: Organised crime in the Netherlands, often referred to as penose, encompasses criminal organizations primarily of Dutch descent active since the 18th century. Based in the Netherlands, these groups are heavily involved in drug trafficking, particularly ecstasy, cocaine, and cannabis, making the Netherlands the world's largest ecstasy exporter. They cooperate with Colombian cartels, British firms, and Moroccan drug barons, and have expanded operations to Spain and Thailand. The term penose originates from Bargoens slang and is used to describe the Dutch underworld.

descriptions: British firms are allied with Penose according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Turkish mafia, Milieu, Penose, Kinahan Cartel, Australian syndicates

descriptions: Penose is listed as an ally of Montenegrin mafia in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Penose , Serbian mafia , 'Ndrangheta , Camorra , Sacra Corona Unita , Colombian drug cartels , Mexican drug cartels

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Norfolk street Bulls, Morse Street County

descriptions: In the TV series Bad Blood, the Rizzuto family is in a feud with the Cosoleto family.
edges.evidence_quote: the Rizzuto crime family ... becomes embroiled in a bloody mafia feud with two powerful 'Ndrangheta families: the Cosoleto crime family ... and the Langana crime family

descriptions: The Spook Hunters attacked black youths, leading to the formation of the Gladiators as a defensive response.
edges.evidence_quote: In response to these white racist gangs, the black youths formed their own gangs to protect themselves and their neighborhoods: The Slausons, the Businessmen, and the Gladiators were all formed as a direct reaction to attacks from white gangs like the Spook Hunters.

descriptions: Wagner Group allied with FAMa.

descriptions: The Outlaws recruited former members of the Night Sinners.

descriptions: The Bubbo killed a member of the Trataculo, leading to a retaliation attempt by the Trataculo against the Bubbo.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 4 febbraio 2000 viene ucciso a Petronà Alberto Carpino della 'ndrina dei Trataculo. Come ritorsione, nel 2001, due loro affiliati ... sarebbero stati in procinto di andare a Galbiate per eliminare Filippo Bubbo

descriptions: Hezbollah fighters clashed with Future Movement militiamen in 2008 street battles.
edges.evidence_quote: Hezbollah-led opposition fighters seized control of several West Beirut neighbourhoods from Future Movement militiamen loyal to the backed government, in street battles that left 11 dead and 30 wounded.

descriptions: Future Movement official criticized Hezbollah, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: An official of the Future Movement, part of the March 14 Alliance, warned that Hezbollah 'has all the characteristics of a terrorist party'

edges.target: Front for the Advancement and Progress of Haïti
descriptions: FRAPH is described as modern Tonton Macoutes, indicating it is a successor or continuation.
edges.evidence_quote: The most feared paramilitary group during the 1990s was the Front for the Advancement and Progress of Haïti (FRAPH), which Toronto Star journalist Linda Diebel described as modern Tonton Macoutes

descriptions: Chotu gang is a principal gang within the Katcha dacoits.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable members: Principal gangs: Chotu gang , Nazroo gang , Ladi gang

descriptions: Kutaisi Clan is a major criminal clan within the Georgian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Georgian mafia has two major criminal clans from Tbilisi and Kutaisi.

descriptions: Kutaisi and Tbilisi clans are in a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: In more recent years, the Georgian underworld in Russia, Georgia and elsewhere has been characterized by a violent feud between the Kutaisi and Tbilisi clans, led by Tariel Oniani and Aslan Usoyan, respectively.

Henry Street Gang.description: The Henry Street Gang was a Chicago street gang of the late nineteenth century, formed by Chris Merry in the early 1890s. Based in Chicago's Southwest Side, they committed armed robbery against local merchants and street peddlers, often posing as push cart vendors. The gang developed a robbery method known as the 'kick in' and disappeared within months after Merry was hanged for murdering his wife.

descriptions: Remo Lecce Libera claims independence except from 'Ndrangheta, implying cooperation or subordination.
edges.evidence_quote: Remo Lecce Libera: formed by some leading criminal figures from Lecce, who claim to be independent from any criminal group other than the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: Remo Lecce Libera opposed Neapolitan Camorra penetration in Salento.
edges.evidence_quote: La Remo Lecce Libera richiedeva infatti l'indipendenza dei criminali leccesi da qualsiasi organizzazione criminale che non fosse la 'Ndrangheta ma venne stroncata sul nascere dalla Sacra Corona Unita, che minacciò pesantemente di uccidere i suoi affiliati, come infatti avvenne con l'omicidio di Donato Erpete nel 1989.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Unita suppressed Remo Lecce Libera, threatening and killing its affiliates.
edges.evidence_quote: La Remo Lecce Libera richiedeva infatti l'indipendenza dei criminali leccesi da qualsiasi organizzazione criminale che non fosse la 'Ndrangheta ma venne stroncata sul nascere dalla Sacra Corona Unita, che minacciò pesantemente di uccidere i suoi affiliati, come infatti avvenne con l'omicidio di Donato Erpete nel 1989.

descriptions: Remo Lecce Libera is an antagonistic group that formed due to internal difficulties of SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Remo Lecce Libera : formed by some leading criminal figures from Lecce, who claim to be independent from any criminal group other than the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: Issei Boys is a sub-group of Tokyo Boyz.
edges.evidence_quote: They were split into three groups/ranks, The Kibei Boys (Japanese-Americans from Japan), Issei Boys (1st generation Japanese-Americans), and the Nisei Boys (2nd generation Japanese-Americans).

descriptions: Young Gooch is listed as an ally of Gooch Close Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Young Gooch, Mossway, Longsight Street Soldiers, Pit Bull Crew, Fallowfield Man Dem, Fallowfield Mad Dogs, Old Trafford Crips, Rusholme Crew Gangsters, Rusholme Crips, M21, RangeBlock, Noonan Crime Family, Cheetham Hill Gang, Shower Posse, British Crime Firms

descriptions: Young Gooch is an offshoot of Gooch Close Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1996, an offshoot gang was identified (Young Gooch)

descriptions: Young Gooch was in conflict with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: The Young Gooch started to war with the newly emerging Longsight Crew

descriptions: The Rhodes Gang was an ally of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club, Parlor Mob, Gorillas, Rhodes Gang

descriptions: The Maesano family was part of a broader criminal association that included the Favasuli family.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan nasce nella zona di Roghudi e Roccaforte del Greco , come parte di una più ampia associazione criminale formata dalle famiglie Maesano, Pangallo, Verno e Favasuli.
